Tests the behavior of JavaScript prototypes with modifications and freezing.

# Purpose
This code modifies built-in JavaScript prototypes and applies specific operations to them. It adds the `Math.sin` function to the `Array.prototype` using the `push` method, which allows arrays to use `Math.sin` as a method. The `Object.freeze` method is then used to make the `Array.prototype` immutable, preventing further modifications to its properties. Additionally, the code calls the `match` method on `String.prototype`, passing itself as an argument, which is a self-referential operation. This code provides narrow functionality by altering and freezing prototypes, which can affect all instances of arrays and strings in the JavaScript environment.
